title: val/prop grammar for the kinds cluster (follows #909 spec)
manager: The val/prop grammar for state "kinds" — the per-state visual-status predicates (terminal, error, active) that the #909 umbrella defines — pending that spec landing first. This is the language-surface half of the kinds feature: once #909 settles what a kind is, this is the grammar that lets you declare a kind and query it. Deliberately blocked on the spec, so the grammar doesn't get built against a definition that then changes.
cs: grammar for kinds val/prop per the #909 spec (needs spec first); fsl#1256 (state field kind) / #1257 (operator kind); see the state-kinds umbrella #909 and the `state * terminal: {}` canonical form.
